title: TestFlight
objectives: Distribute iOS beta builds via TestFlight
Automate uploads via Fastlane pilot / Codemagic

sections.markdown: **TestFlight** is Apple's official beta distribution for iOS/iPadOS/macOS. Internal (up to 100 users, instant) or external (up to 10,000 users, requires a light beta app review) — with expiry (typically 90 days), feedback collection, and crash logs.

TestFlight's edge: real App Store install experience, real crash logs to Xcode + Crashlytics, and a **feedback surface built into iOS**. Combined with Fastlane's `pilot` you get one-command uploads: `fastlane pilot upload`.
Not filling in beta app review info (test account, contact) — Apple rejects external builds for missing info faster than for real issues. Also: 90-day expiry surprises testers — plan renewal builds.
Segment testers into groups (QA, marketing, VIP customers) and target releases per group. Auto-notify testers of new builds. Ask for feedback in-app for high-signal input.

flashcards.back: Internal: ≤100 team members, no review. External: ≤10,000 users, requires beta review.
Typically 90 days from build upload — plan renewal builds.
fastlane pilot upload / Codemagic / GitHub Actions actions.

quiz.explain: Apple reviews beta builds too, faster.

challenges.text: Upload a build to TestFlight via App Store Connect.
Segment testers into 'qa' + 'beta'; target a release to one group.
Automate uploads via Fastlane pilot in CI.
Auto-promote a nightly TestFlight build to external testers after N days of clean stability.

interviewQuestions: How do you automate TestFlight uploads?
How do you gather beta feedback?
